content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcer PnecrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street e Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA g5gl4_2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322_0886
December 14,2005
ElizabethWagner Hull
Assistant City Attorney
City of Chula Vista
276Fourth Street
Chula Vista, CA 91910
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-05-233
Dear Ms. Hull:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Chula Vista
Councilmember Jerrl' Rindone regarding tlie conflict-of-interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the ,,Act,).r
QUESTION
May Councilmember Rindone participate in a decision to appropriate additional
funds necessary to meet the City's existing contractual obligations -ittr r"gurd to the
Civic Center Complex construction and remodel pro.iect due to the incapac-itation of
another city councilmember?
CONCLUSION
Yes' Councilmember Rindone may participate in the decision to appropriate
additional funds for the Civic Center Complix undier the exception for t"gutiy required
participation.
FACTS
The City of Chula Vista is a charter city governed by a council, consisting of four
council members and a mayor, elected from the iity at-largi. rrr" city charlerfrovides
that appropriations made after the adoption of the budget r:equire the affirmativ'e votes of
I Government Code sections 8 1 000 - gl0l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
A[r-+1


File No. A-05-233
Page No. 2
at least four members. The city budget is adopted in June of each year. The adoption of
resolutions and ordinances require three affirmative votes.
In a December 5-,2005 phone call, you stated that Councilmember pattyDavis
became seriously ill and was unable to periorm her duties as of July 29,2005. since that
time, she has been temporarily incapaciiated and has been unable to participate in any
council meetings. Her return date is not known at this time.
- 
The city of chula vista is currently in phase 2 of the3-phase Civic Center
complex construction and remodel proj""i. The city council approved a dlsign-build
agreement for all three phases of construction in February 2ooa: At the time Lf the vote,
based upon phone advice received from our ageflcy,councilmembe, Jeol.Rindorre
recused himself' The councilmember Rindone o*r. u home within 500 feet of the civic
center complex' The council member has not participitid in any actionsreiated to the
Civic Center Complex project.
Pursuant to the existing design-build agreement between the city and its
contractor, Highland partnership, certain bills-are coming due. In *0", t" prvirr"
outstanding invoices the city council will need to appropiiate additional funds to the
project' As of December I the project will be out of runas and have outstanding bills to
be paid' 
- Based upon the abovelnformation, of the five council members, only three
appeat able, at the time, to participate in a decision related to the civic Center complex.
An appropriation will require foui affirmative votes.
You wish to know whether the legally required participation exception applies,
therefore allowing councilmember Rindon"io pu.ti"id;i" the decision to appropriate
additional funds necessary to meet the city,s e*istirgiontractuat obligations.
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will ,.perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests
or the financial interests of persons who .h1ve supported i}r"..,, (Section g 1 001 (b).)
section 87100 prohibits anypubric officiar from-makin g, j*ti"iputing in making, or
gtheryrse using his or her official position to influencei'fo""*ental decision in which
the official has a financial interest. 
- -'a-
The commission has adopted an_eight-step standard analysis for deciding whether
an official has a disqualiSring conflict of iriterest.')6"grtuiion 1g700(b).) your question
2 Regulation t:700q) sets out the eight-step process: To determine whether a given individual
has a disqualitring conflict of interest under tL r9y,31ryqr- e"i, !ro"""d with the 6uo*irg anatysis:
(1) Determine whether the individual is a public official; fzl o"te.-iri whether the official wiil be making,
participating in making, or using gl attgmfting to use his/irer officiar fosition to influence a government
decisioq (3) Identiff the public official's ecoiomic inleresr.; f+j o.,ir-ine whether that intlrest is directly
or indirectly involved in the govemmental decision; (s) oetermine the-appticaule materiality standard; (6)


File No. A-05-233
Page No. 3
only concerns the last step, whether Councilmember Rindone may participate in a
governmental decision under the "legally required participation" exception. We assume,
for purposes of this request for advice, that Councilmember Rindone lias a conflict-of-
interest.
Legally Requtred Participatton:
The Act recognizes that there may be instances where a goveflrmental body
cannot function without the participation of a public official who has a conflict of
interest. The Act contains a narrow exception to the conflict-of-interest rules codified at
Section 87101.
Section 87101 allows an official, who is otherwise disqualified to particip atein a
govemmental decision, if the official's participation is "legally required." The exception
is narrowly construed and applies only where there is no "alternative source of decision
consistent with the pu{poses and terms of the statute authoizingthe decision.,,
(Regulation 18708(a)(c); In re Tobias (t999) 13 Fppc ops. 5.) Under regularion
18708(d), where a supermajority is required, the necessary quorum for decision-making
is "the minimum number ofmembers needed for that adoption.,,
For instance, we have advised that apublic official's habitual absence from
meetings was not sufficient to create the requisite legal necessity for another public
official's participation in the decision. (Cohen Advice Letter, A-94-274; Kimbrell
Advice Letter, No. 4-97-201.) On the other hand, we have also advised,thatone of two
disqualified councilmembers could participate in a governmental decision because it was
otherwise impossible to achieve a quorum due to the absence of an incap acitated
councilmember who was undergoing a liver transplant. (Wynder Advice Letter,A-gl-
166.) The Wynder analysis appears to apply to your facts.
Under the facts you have presented, the Civic Center Complex construction and
rernodel project is a critical project to the city. It is currently in phase 2 of a3-phase
conskuction and remodel project. A delay in the decision to appropriate funds would
result in a delay in the completion of the project. A supermajority of four of the five
councilmembers is required to make appropriations to the project. Furthermore, it is not
possible to obtain a quorum to make this decision, because of Councilmember Davis,
serious illness, and there exists no alternative source of decision other than the city
council. You also stated that there existed no alternative method ofmaking the dicision,
such as by appointing a temporary councilmember.
Based on the specific facts you have presented, Councilmember Rindone may
participate in the appropriation decision. (Dorsey Advice Letter,No. A-92-0g9.)
Deterrnine whether it is reasonably foreseeable that the governmental decision will have a material
financial effect on the economic interest; (7) Determine if the reasonably foreseeable f,rancial effect is
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally; and (8) Determine if the public official,s
participation is legally required despite the conflict of interest.


File No. A-05-233
Page No. 4
However, please note that while Councilmember Rindon emayparticipate fully in
public deliberations regarding the matter , and atclosed sessions required by law,he may
not attempt to influence the outcome of the matter ,.behind tt " ,."*#; ilyl"grgirrg in
private discussions with other members or staff. (GrunwaldAdvice r"t"., N-". A-95-
184; Romney Advice Lette4 No. 4-99-263.) Also, he mu.t disclose on the record the
existence his financial interest, describe with particularitf the nature of the economic
interest giving rise to the conflict, and,disclose ttre tegat ba;is for concluaing tt at there is
no altemative source of decision. (Regulation lAZOg[b).)
,rr_rul[!'u 
have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division
ER:rd
